## Cases mentioned in passing

### Sapphire instrument consulting project
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Sapphire instrument consulting project"
- **Anchor:** `§10.p4`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "Another way to get something stiffer is to take it and turn it into a tube. *[Tom holds up a piece of polyethylene tubing.]* This is polyethylene. It's a tube. It's stiffer because it has a geometric stiffness. Your mechanical engineers, you work on geometry to get stiffness; materials engineers like to pick a better material. But there are limits to the materials we can have. So I told them that what they could make their tools out of — if you look on this list for material selection, alumina, sapphire — I said, why don't you make sapphire tools? You could buy sapphire for a couple of bucks..."
